params.name: Head of Bhairava
params.description:
On Apr 20, 1518 AD, Head of Bhairava was created and enshrined on the altar.
Head of Bhairava is a mask made of gilt copper with rock crystal and paint during Nepal's Malla period. It is a terrifying manifestation of Hindu god Shiva. Legend has it that Brahma and Vishnu engaged in an argument about who's the supreme deity of the universe. Brahma considered himself the supreme deity. Shiva in a rage threw a nail of his finger and it turned to Bhairava. Bhairava cut off the fifth head of Brahma …
